paragraphs: Harmonics are an essential topic in a power system, as they affect both the quality of the electricity and the performance of the apparatus.

High harmonic levels may cause overheating of components and reduce the device’s lifespan. Pure sinusoidal waves generated by a perfect power source are warped by harmonics. Harmonic distortion hinders a company’s capacity for production and operation. Here are some economic effects of harmonic distortion.

Nonlinear loads that draw current in irregular, brief spurts generate harmonics. Due to the interference caused by these vibrations, it is possible for harmonic currents to travel freely throughout the entire power system . In industrial settings, harmonics reduce the efficacy and quality of electricity. The preponderance of commercial electrical systems can safely support nonlinear loads of up to 15%. Negative harmonic distortions may occur if the percentage of nonlinear loading exceeds 15%. Consequently, it is essential to monitor harmonic levels to ensure they remain within the allowed ranges. Thus, harmonic research and analysis aid in maintaining knowledge of potential risks and their fundamental causes, making it simpler to prevent harmonic-related issues during the design phase and decreasing the likelihood of unintended outcomes .
Despite the minimal likelihood of harmonic issues, a punctual harmonic analysis must be performed to ensure that the harmonic levels are appropriate. These are the harmonic level progressions:
